d=json.load(sys.stdin)

"

Let me locate the precedent AML docs to anchor retention/classification, and check Linear + any existing clearance doc.

cat > /tmp/aml_memo.txt << 'EOF'
ACME CLOUD - AML / FinCEN WIRE-MONITORING CLEARANCE MEMO
=========================================================
Case reference : JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (Oracle GL id je_b2c2b939a1244823)
Entity         : Acme Cloud
Fiscal period  : acme_cloud_FP-2026-04
Prepared by    : Marina Soko, Compliance Officer
Date           : 2026-06-12
Classification : RESTRICTED   |   Retention: AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7 years)

1. ALERT TRIGGER
   The firm's standing FinCEN wire-monitoring threshold ($10,000) fired on the
   2026-04-22 cash posting in account 101000 Cash - Operating. The underlying
   item is a settlement receipt from Acme Cloud's largest enterprise SaaS
   customer.

2. LEDGER / CASH-POSTING CONSISTENCY CHECK
   JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 posts:
       DR 101000 Cash - Operating ............ $57,077.69
       CR 110000 Accounts Receivable - Trade . $57,077.69
   Source module AR, standard entry; prepared by owen.mercer, approved/posted by
   edith.banda; posted 2026-04-22. The cash lines clear an existing trade
   receivable - i.e., collection of contracted SaaS revenue - which is fully
   consistent with the documented CDD rationale (source of funds = contracted
   SaaS revenue received on the documented schedule). The wire confirmation
   reference, standing payment instructions, and the JE amount all tie out.
   CONCLUSION: cash posting lines are consistent with the CDD rationale; no
   discrepancy.

3. CUSTOMER DUE DILIGENCE REVIEW (analyst pass - Farah Dlamini, AML Analyst)
   - Counterparty aligns to the beneficial-owner file.
   - Jurisdiction and UBO chain match onboarding documentation.
     Cross-references: "Acme Cloud FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh"
     (doc_fb028c9124e146c5) and "Acme Cloud FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo"
     (doc_38a8236a0c4546e2).
   - Source of funds: contracted SaaS revenue on the documented schedule.
   - Wire confirmation reference aligns to the JE amount and standing payment
     instructions.

4. DISPOSITION
   CLEAR - No SAR filed. No suspicious-activity indicators identified.
   Supervisory gate approval : Anita Knowles
   Partner approval          : Steven Perry (Managing Partner)
   Case thread opened 2026-04-23 and formally closed in
   #compliance-and-registrations on 2026-05-04.

5. RECORDS / RETENTION TREATMENT
   Filed as RESTRICTED with AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7-year) retention, anchored to the
   firm's existing AML precedent for this client (FY2026 Beneficial Owner
   Refresh and FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo, both restricted / AICPA_SQMS_7Y).

6. OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UP
   - Stale AML threshold-alert reminder (reminder_scen_041_audit_compliance_0000)
     cleared on file close.
   - Next standing control: Acme Cloud FY2027 BO refresh + AML risk
     re-assessment, kickoff 2027-04-13 (recurring annual control already set).
   No open compliance items remain on this case. File is fully closed on the
   compliance side.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/aml_memo.txt

Matthew, Steven,

Quick confirmation ahead of the partner review cycle: the Acme Cloud AML monitoring file tied to JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (Oracle GL id je_b2c2b939a1244823) is now fully closed out on the compliance side. Tagging the JE in the subject so you can correlate it against the original FinCEN wire-monitoring alert.

Background: the standing $10,000 wire-monitoring threshold fired on the 2026-04-22 settlement receipt from Acme's largest enterprise SaaS customer, posted to account 101000 Cash - Operating.

What I verified and actioned:
• Ledger consistency — the JE posts DR 101000 Cash - Operating $57,077.69 / CR 110000 Accounts Receivable - Trade $57,077.69. The cash lines clear an existing trade receivable (collection of contracted SaaS revenue), which is fully consistent with the documented CDD rationale — source of funds, wire confirmation reference, standing payment instructions, and amount all tie out. No discrepancy.
• Disposition — CLEARED with no SAR; Farah ran the analyst pass (counterparty/BO/UBO/source-of-funds all aligned to the onboarding and BO files), with Anita's supervisory approval and Steven's partner approval complete.
• Housekeeping closed — I cleared a stale standing AML threshold-alert reminder that was still open after clearance, and filed the AML/CDD clearance memo to the Records Vault (doc_90af987949b8), classified RESTRICTED with 7-year AICPA_SQMS_7Y retention, anchored to our existing AML precedent for this client (FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh and FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo) and linked to the JE.
• Forward control — the FY2027 BO refresh + AML risk re-assessment is already on the standing control calendar (kickoff 2027-04-13).

No open compliance items remain on this case.

Thanks,
Marina

Marina Soko
Compliance Officer, Brookfield CPAs & Advisors

content.text:
Done. Here's the wrap-up on the Acme Cloud AML file.

## What I found

**The case** — AML monitoring case for **JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052** (Oracle GL id `je_b2c2b939a1244823`). The standing $10K FinCEN wire-monitoring threshold fired on the 2026-04-22 settlement receipt from Acme's largest enterprise SaaS customer. Farah ran the analyst pass; cleared with Anita (supervisory) and Steven (partner) sign-off.

**Ledger / cash-posting check — consistent.** The JE posts `DR 101000 Cash – Operating $57,077.69 / CR 110000 AR – Trade $57,077.69` (AR module, standard entry). The cash lines clear an existing trade receivable — i.e., collection of contracted SaaS revenue — which exactly matches the documented CDD rationale (source of funds = contracted SaaS revenue on the documented schedule; wire confirmation reference, standing payment instructions, and amount all tie out). **No discrepancy.**

**Two things were still sitting open** on our side despite the clearance being done:
1. A **stale standing AML threshold-alert reminder** (`reminder_scen_041_audit_compliance_0000`) was still live even though the case had been cleared.
2. **Documentation gap** — the only Records Vault doc tied to the JE was the routine AR accounting support (internal). There was **no AML/CDD clearance memo** on file documenting the review and disposition.

## What I actioned
- **Deleted the stale reminder.**
- **Filed the AML/CDD clearance memo** to the Records Vault (**`doc_90af987949b8`**), linked to the JE. Treatment **anchored to the firm's existing AML precedent for this client** — the FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh and FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo, both **RESTRICTED / AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7-year)** — so the new memo carries the same classification and retention (expires 2033).
- **Posted a recap** under the case thread in #compliance-and-registrations.
- **Emailed Matthew Li and Steven Perry** (CC Farah), with the JE tagged in the subject so they can correlate against the original alert, confirming the file is fully closed on the compliance side.

The FY2027 BO refresh + AML re-assessment is already on the standing control calendar (kickoff 2027-04-13), so the forward control is in place. Nothing else is outstanding — the file is fully closed.

One note for transparency: the Records Vault upload didn't carry an `entity_id` field on the resulting record (the upload endpoint doesn't take that parameter), but the memo is firmly linked to the Acme Cloud JE via `related_resource_id` and titled accordingly, so it surfaces correctly against the case.
